>How will MDTs respond to creates in the wrong stripe?

I think we trust client to choose MDT correctly, and also the MDT supposes to treat single stripe and normal directory equally.

Patch Set 11: Code-Review+1

From sanity.sh in #9:
	
>	John L. Hammond		10:52 AM

> (defect) With OBD_FAIL_UNKNOWN_LMV_STRIPE still set, we need to (try to) lookup and unlink some files that are known not to exist. Please use a different file name for each operation so that there are no negative dentry caching effects.
	
			
> 	wangdi		11:13 AM

> hmm, I did not follow your point here. we are trying to access the unknown hash type file here. And there is umount and mount later in the following. Do I miss sth?

In the sanity test we check to see that we can lookup and unlink existing files in a directory with unknown hash type. We should *also* check that trying to lookup or unlink a file that does not actually exist is handled sanely. More generally if we allow clients to handle directories with unknown hash types then we should check that all operations are handled sanely.

parents.subject: LU-5270 obdclass: do not lock repetitively when geting jobid

commit.subject: LU-4921 lmv: try all stripes for unknown hash functions
commit.message:
LU-4921 lmv: try all stripes for unknown hash functions

For unknown hash type, LMV should try all stripes to locate
the name entry. But it will only for lookup and unlink, i.e.
we can only list and unlink entries under striped dir with
unknown hash type.

Signed-off-by: wang di <di.wang@intel.com>
Change-Id: Ifeed7131c24e48277a6cc8fd4c09b7534e31079f
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/10041
Tested-by: Jenkins
Reviewed-by: John L. Hammond <john.hammond@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <andreas.dilger@intel.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
